page: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is configured with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 22,500 GB NVMe SSD array. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and cryptographic capabilities. The instance achieved a strong multi-core stress-ng performance score of 92,861.79 and a top-tier Passmark CPU encryption score of 87,228.27. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block read speeds reaching 4,969.98 GB/sec and large-block write speeds at 139.48 GB/sec. Database operations are highly performant, as demonstrated by a strong Passmark database score of 29,810.64 and Redis SET operations executing at 89.14 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and large-model LLM text generation represent relative performance limitations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i4i.24xlarge offers high resource density by combining a massive local NVMe SSD storage capacity with a substantial memory footprint, reducing the need for external network-attached storage. This hardware profile is highly suited for I/O-intensive workloads, large-scale relational and non-relational databases, high-throughput Redis caching tiers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features a bundled 22,500 GB NVMe SSD storage array and a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database execution, highlighted by a Passmark database score of 29,810.64 and Redis SET throughput of 89.14 million operations per second. While single-core speeds and large-scale LLM inference are moderate, the instance provides high qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput caching, and intensive multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and a bundled 22,500 GB NVMe SSD array designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 96 Intel vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and high-throughput multi-core processing for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 22,500 GB high-speed NVMe SSD local storage
Strong multi-core processing and high-speed memory bandwidth
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and I/O-intensive workloads
